title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
text: dimsprepr provides complete, reproducible preprocessing pipeline direct-injection mass spectrometry (DIMS) metabolomics data. covers: missing-value filtering, imputation, signal-drift batch correction, normalization, transformation, scaling, quality filtering — orchestrated single function call.

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
code: library(dimsprepr)  # Load the bundled example dataset data(dims_data, package = "dimsprepr") data(dims_metadata, package = "dimsprepr")  dim(dims_data) #> [1] 50 80 table(dims_metadata$Group) #>  #>   Control        QC Treatment  #>        20        10        20

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
text: run_DIpreprocess() function executes preprocessing steps fixed, validated order. skip drift correction (since small simulated dataset without realistic QC injection pattern): result named list intermediate final data matrices:
code: result <- run_DIpreprocess(   x                = dims_data,   metadata         = dims_metadata,   normalize_method = "median",   transform_method = "log10",   correct_drift    = FALSE,   rsd_threshold    = 0.5,   variance_percentile = 5,   verbose          = FALSE )  result #> === run_DIpreprocess pipeline result === #> STATUS : OK #> Data   : 50 samples x 36 features (final) #> Time   : 4.8 second(s) # Final preprocessed matrix (auto-scaled for general analysis) dim(result$data_nonpls) #> [1] 50 36  # Step-by-step dimension tracking result$dimensions #>                                           Step Samples Features #> 1                                     Original      50       80 #> 2                  After zero -> NA conversion      50       78 #> 3                         After missing filter      50       77 #> 4                       After drift correction      50       77 #> 5       [median_log10] After NONPLS RSD filter      50       44 #> 6  [median_log10] After NONPLS variance filter      50       38 #> 7          [median_log10] After PLS RSD filter      50       44 #> 8     [median_log10] After PLS variance filter      50       41 #> 9                [median_log10] Final (NONPLS)      50       36 #> 10                  [median_log10] Final (PLS)      50       36

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
text: preprocessing step also available standalone function maximum flexibility.

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
code: filt <- run_filtermissing(   dims_data,   dims_metadata,   threshold = 0.3,   verbose   = FALSE ) filt #> === Missing Value Filtering Results === #> Features before: 80 #> Features after:  77 #> Features removed: 3 (3.8%) #> Threshold: 30.0% #> Mode: Any group(s) must pass #> Zeros converted to NA: 342

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
code: imp <- run_mvimpute(filt$data, method = 0.2, verbose = FALSE) imp #> === Missing Value Imputation Results === #> Method: Deterministic: 0.2000 x min positive #> Missing values before: 195 #> Missing values after:  0

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
code: norm <- run_normalize(imp$data, dims_metadata, method = "median", verbose = FALSE)

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
code: trans <- run_transform(norm$data, method = "log10", verbose = FALSE)

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
code: scaled <- run_scale(trans$data, method = "auto", verbose = FALSE) scaled #> === Scaling Results === #> Method  : auto #> Data    : 50 samples x 77 features

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr
text: Supply vectors normalize_method /transform_method compute combinations automatically. Expensive upstream steps (filtering, imputation, drift correction) run :
code: multi <- run_DIpreprocess(   x                = dims_data,   metadata         = dims_metadata,   normalize_method = c("sum", "median"),   transform_method = c("log10", "log2"),   correct_drift    = FALSE,   verbose          = FALSE ) names(multi) # [1] "sum_log10"    "sum_log2"     "median_log10" "median_log2"

title: Getting Started with dimsprepr

title: dimsprepr • Statistical Analysis Made Easier
text: dimsprepr provides complete, reproducible preprocessing pipeline direct-injection mass spectrometry (DIMS) metabolomics data. Built around single orchestrating function, package executes preprocessing steps — outlier removal, missing value filtering, imputation, signal drift batch correction, normalization, transformation, scaling, quality filtering — fixed, validated order, ensuring methodological consistency across studies. key feature support Cartesian product pipelines: users may supply multiple normalization transformation methods simultaneously, computationally expensive upstream steps executed shared across method combinations. enables systematic, side--side comparison preprocessing strategies within single function call. dimsprepr designed metabolomics researchers need structured, auditable preprocessing workflow ready downstream statistical analysis.

title: dimsprepr • Statistical Analysis Made Easier
text: can install development version dimsprepr directly GitHub:
code: # Install pak if you haven't already install.packages("pak")  # Install the GitHub package pak::pak("jllcalorio/dimsprepr")

what: Example Direct-Injection Metabolomics Data Matrix — dims_data
title: Example Direct-Injection Metabolomics Data Matrix — dims_data
text: simulated numeric matrix 50 samples x 80 features representing raw peak intensities direct-injection mass spectrometry experiment. Rows samples; columns features (m/z bins). Zero values represent -detection-limit measurements. Features 78-80 intentionally sparse exercise missing-value filtering.

title: Example Direct-Injection Metabolomics Data Matrix — dims_data
code: dims_data

title: Example Direct-Injection Metabolomics Data Matrix — dims_data
text: numeric matrix 50 rows 80 columns. Row names sample identifiers (S01–S50); column names feature labels (mz_0001–mz_0080).

title: Example Direct-Injection Metabolomics Data Matrix — dims_data
text: Simulated data. See data-raw/dims_example.R generation script.

title: Example Direct-Injection Metabolomics Data Matrix — dims_data
code: data(dims_data) dim(dims_data) #> [1] 50 80

what: Example Sample Metadata for dims_data — dims_metadata
title: Example Sample Metadata for dims_data — dims_metadata
text: data frame sample-level metadata matching rows dims_data. Contains group labels, batch assignments, injection order, subject identifiers.

title: Example Sample Metadata for dims_data — dims_metadata
code: dims_metadata

title: Example Sample Metadata for dims_data — dims_metadata
text: data frame 50 rows 5 columns: Sample Character. Unique sample identifier (S01–S50). Group Character. Sample group: "Control", "Treatment", "QC". Batch Integer. Batch number (1 2). InjectionSequence Integer. Order injection (1–50). SubjectID Character. Biological subject ID non-QC samples; NA QC.

title: Example Sample Metadata for dims_data — dims_metadata
text: Simulated data. See data-raw/dims_example.R.

title: Example Sample Metadata for dims_data — dims_metadata
code: data(dims_metadata) table(dims_metadata$Group) #>  #>   Control        QC Treatment  #>        20        10        20

what: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: Generates multi-panel plot comparing signal selected features data transformation, correction, manipulation. panel displays one feature across injection sequence, colored sample group shaped batch, optional LOESS trend line fitted user-defined reference group (e.g., QC samples).

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
code: plot_beforeafter(   x,   y,   metadata,   plot_what = NULL,   col_injection = "InjectionSequence",   col_batch = "Batch",   col_group = "Group",   col_qc_label = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   arrange_group = NULL,   plot_title = NULL,   x_label = "Before",   y_label = "After",   log_transform = TRUE,   point_size = 2,   point_alpha = 0.7,   plot_cols = NULL,   theme = "nature",   base_size = 15,   font_family = "sans",   seed = 123,   global_font_size = NULL,   title_size = NULL,   subtitle_size = NULL,   xlab_size = NULL,   ylab_size = NULL )

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: x data.frame, tibble, matrix representing data transformation. Rows samples; columns features. Column names may contain special characters. Input can link{run_DIpreprocess} class. y data.frame, tibble, matrix representing data transformation. Must dimensions column names x. metadata data.frame containing sample-level metadata shared x y. Must number rows x y. plot_what Character vector NULL. Column names x plotted. NULL (default), function randomly selects 6 features. specific features provided found, warning issued skipped. none provided features found, function falls back random selection. col_injection Character. Name column metadata containing injection sequence (numeric order runs). Default "InjectionSequence". run_DIpreprocess class, value automatically taken $parameters$injection_col. col_batch Character. Name column metadata containing batch identifiers. Default "Batch". run_DIpreprocess class, value automatically taken $parameters$batch_col. col_group Character. Name column metadata containing sample group labels (e.g., "QC", "Sample"). Default "Group". run_DIpreprocess class, value automatically taken $parameters$group_col. col_qc_label Character vector. value(s) col_group column identifies reference group LOESS trend fitting (typically QC samples). Can vector like c("SQC", "EQC"). NULL, LOESS line drawn. Default c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). run_DIpreprocess class, value automatically taken $parameters$qc_types. arrange_group Character vector NULL. Custom order sample groups col_group. NULL (default), groups arranged alphanumerically, col_qc_label values placed first. plot_title Character NULL. Main title combined plot. NULL (default), title automatically generated based x_label, y_label, number batches. x_label Character. Label -correction facet panel. Default "". y_label Character. Label -correction facet panel. Default "". log_transform Logical. TRUE, applies log10 transformation (small floor 1e-10) feature values plotting. Default TRUE. point_size Numeric. Size individual data points. Default 2. point_alpha Numeric [0, 1]. Transparency data points. Default 0.7. plot_cols Integer NULL. Number columns combined plot grid. NULL, determined automatically. Default NULL. theme Character. ggplot2 theme apply panel. Options: "nature", "minimal", "classic", "bw", "light", "dark". Default "nature" (clean, publication-ready style based theme_bw()). base_size Numeric. Base font size theme (pts). Default 15. font_family Character. Font family text elements. Default "sans". seed Numeric NULL. Random seed passed set.seed() internal sampling operations. Default 123. global_font_size Numeric NULL. Base font size proportional scaling text elements. Default NULL. title_size Numeric NULL. Feature panel title font size. Default NULL. subtitle_size Numeric NULL. Main plot title (top grob) font size. Default NULL. xlab_size Numeric NULL. X-axis label font size. Default NULL. ylab_size Numeric NULL. Y-axis label font size. Default NULL.

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: Invisibly returns gtable grob object (gridExtra::arrangeGrob()) containing feature panels. plot also drawn active graphics device.

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: Plot Pairwise --Comparison Selected Features x y must share identical row counts, column counts, column names. Row order assumed correspond x, y, metadata. log_transform = TRUE, values floored 1e-10 log transformation avoid -Inf results zero negative values. Font sizes resolved following priority (highest lowest): explicit size argument (e.g., title_size) > global_font_size scaling > base_size-derived default. global_font_size provided specific size , text elements scale proportionally relative base size 11. LOESS trend line fitted group identified col_qc_label. fewer 3 observations group present, warning issued line suppressed feature. combined plot rendered via gridExtra::arrangeGrob() displayed grid::grid.draw(). returned object invisible gtable grob can redrawn exported ggplot2::ggsave().

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Plot Pairwise Before-and-After Comparison for Selected Features — plot_beforeafter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) n_samples  <- 30 n_features <- 4  x <- as.data.frame(   matrix(abs(rnorm(n_samples * n_features, mean = 1000, sd = 200)),          nrow = n_samples,          dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("Feature_", seq_len(n_features)))) ) y <- as.data.frame(x + rnorm(n_samples * n_features, sd = 50))  meta <- data.frame(   InjectionSequence = seq_len(n_samples),   Batch             = rep(c("B1", "B2"), each = n_samples / 2),   Group             = rep(c("Sample", "QC"), times = c(n_samples - 6, 6)) )  plot_beforeafter(   x             = x,   y             = y,   metadata      = meta,   plot_what     = c("Feature_1", "Feature_2"),   col_injection = "InjectionSequence",   col_batch     = "Batch",   col_group     = "Group",   col_qc_label  = "QC",   theme         = "nature" ) } # }

what: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: Generates density box plots comparing data distributions transformation, normalization, scaling, correction step. Plots can oriented sample feature. Returns named list individual ggplot2 objects combined gtable grob.

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
code: plot_dist_beforeafter(   x,   y,   group_by = "sample",   plot_what = NULL,   n_random = 30L,   x_label = "Before",   y_label = "After",   plot_title = NULL,   x_fill = "#56B4E9",   y_fill = "#E69F00",   point_alpha = 0.6,   theme = "nature",   base_size = 15,   font_family = "sans",   plot_cols = NULL,   seed = 123,   global_font_size = NULL,   title_size = NULL,   subtitle_size = NULL,   xlab_size = NULL,   ylab_size = NULL )

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: x data.frame, tibble, matrix representing data transformation. Rows samples; columns features. Column names may contain special characters. y data.frame, tibble, matrix representing data transformation. Must dimensions column names x. group_by Character. Perspective box plots. "sample" shows one box per sample; "feature" shows one box per feature. Default "sample". plot_what Character vector NULL. Subset column names (features) x y include plots. NULL, features used. Default NULL. n_random Integer NULL. Maximum number samples (group_by = "sample") features (group_by = "feature") display box plots. NULL data fewer items n_random, shown. Default 30. x_label Character. Label describing state, used plot titles. Default "". y_label Character. Label describing state, used plot titles. Default "". plot_title Character NULL. Main title combined plot. NULL (default), title automatically generated. x_fill Character. Fill color density box plots. Default "#56B4E9" (Okabe-Ito sky blue). y_fill Character. Fill color density box plots. Default "#E69F00" (Okabe-Ito orange). point_alpha Numeric [0, 1]. Transparency density fill box fill. Default 0.6. theme Character. ggplot2 theme apply. Options: "nature", "minimal", "classic", "bw", "light", "dark". Default "nature". base_size Numeric. Base font size theme (pts). Default 15. font_family Character. Font family text elements. Default "sans". plot_cols Integer NULL. Number columns combined output grid. NULL, defaults 2 (density row + boxplot row). Default NULL. seed Numeric NULL. Random seed reproducible sampling n_random used. Default 123. global_font_size Numeric NULL. Base font size proportional scaling text elements. Default NULL. title_size Numeric NULL. Plot panel title font size. Default NULL. subtitle_size Numeric NULL. Combined plot main title font size. Default NULL. xlab_size Numeric NULL. X-axis label font size. Default NULL. ylab_size Numeric NULL. Y-axis label font size. Default NULL.

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: named list containing: plot_density_before Density plot x. plot_density_after Density plot y. plot_box_before Box plot x. plot_box_after Box plot y. plot_combined gtable grob four panels, drawn active device.

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: Plot Distribution Comparison Data Transformation x y must share identical row counts, column counts, column names. Row order assumed correspond x y. plot_what specified, features included density box plots. n_random also specified, random sub-sampling applied top plot_what subset box plots ; density plots always use full plot_what subset. Box plot items ordered median value readability. Font sizes follow resolution priority plot_beforeafter: explicit argument > global_font_size scaling > base_size derived default. function renders combined plot active graphics device also returns named list individual panels can extracted, re-styled, exported independently.

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Plot Distribution Comparison Before and After Data Transformation — plot_dist_beforeafter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) n_samples  <- 40 n_features <- 20  x <- as.data.frame(   matrix(abs(rnorm(n_samples * n_features, mean = 1000, sd = 300)),          nrow = n_samples,          dimnames = list(            paste0("S", seq_len(n_samples)),            paste0("Feature_", seq_len(n_features))          )) ) y <- as.data.frame(scale(x))  # Sample-wise comparison, default settings result <- plot_dist_beforeafter(   x       = x,   y       = y,   group_by = "sample" )  # Feature-wise, subset of features, classic theme result <- plot_dist_beforeafter(   x         = x,   y         = y,   group_by  = "feature",   plot_what = paste0("Feature_", 1:10),   theme     = "classic" )  # Extract individual panels result$plot_density_before result$plot_box_after } # }

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: Runs complete, sequential preprocessing pipeline metabolomics data orchestrating individual run_* functions dimsprepr. Steps execute fixed, reproducible order regardless argument arrangement. normalize_method /transform_method supplied character vectors one element, combinations computed.  steps prior normalization (validation, outlier removal, missing-value filtering, imputation, drift/batch correction) executed ; normalization -> transformation -> scaling -> quality filtering -> replicate-merging branch repeated every normalization x transformation pair.  return value case plain list (class "run_DIpreprocess_multi") whose elements named <normalize>_<transform> hold standard run_DIpreprocess object.

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
code: run_DIpreprocess(   x,   metadata,   sample_id_col = "Sample",   group_col = "Group",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   batch_col = "Batch",   injection_col = "InjectionSequence",   norm_factor_col = "Normalization",   subject_id_col = "SubjectID",   outliers = NULL,   missing_threshold = 0.2,   missing_by_group = TRUE,   missing_any_group = TRUE,   missing_include_qc = FALSE,   impute_method = 0.2,   impute_method_post_drift = NULL,   positive_only = TRUE,   correct_drift = TRUE,   remove_uncorrected = FALSE,   spline_smooth = 0,   spline_spar_limit = c(-1.5, 1.5),   correct_on_log = TRUE,   min_qc_per_batch = 5L,   normalize_method = "median",   normalize_ref_sample = NULL,   normalize_qc_method = "none",   transform_method = "log10",   vsn_cores = 1L,   scale_nonpls = "auto",   scale_pls = "pareto",   rsd_threshold = 0.3,   rsd_qc_type = "EQC",   variance_percentile = 10,   scale_filter_ref = "auto",   merge_replicates = FALSE,   track = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: x Matrix data frame. Numeric feature data samples rows features columns. values non-negative raw intensities; zeros treated missing internally. metadata Data frame. Sample metadata nrow(metadata) == nrow(x). minimum, group column (default "Group") must present. sample_id_col Character. Column metadata containing unique sample identifiers match rownames(x). Default: "Sample". group_col Character. Column metadata containing group labels. Default: "Group". qc_types Character vector. Group labels identify QC samples. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). batch_col Character. Column metadata containing batch numbers. Required drift correction. Default: "Batch". injection_col Character. Column metadata containing injection order (integer). Required drift correction. Default: "InjectionSequence". norm_factor_col Character. Column metadata containing external normalization factors. Default: "Normalization". subject_id_col Character. Column metadata containing subject identifiers technical-replicate merging. Default: "SubjectID". outliers Character vector NULL. Row names x remove processing. Default: NULL. missing_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Missing value threshold. Default: 0.2. missing_by_group Logical. TRUE, assess per group. Default: TRUE. missing_any_group Logical. used missing_by_group = TRUE. TRUE (default), feature retained missingness threshold least one group (Modified 80% Rule). FALSE, must satisfy threshold groups. missing_include_qc Logical. TRUE, include QC samples. Default: FALSE. impute_method Numeric > 0. Fraction smallest positive value per feature used imputation. Default: 0.2. impute_method_post_drift Missing value imputation signal drift batch correction. positive_only Logical. TRUE, imputation considers positive values. Default: TRUE. correct_drift Logical. TRUE, apply QCRSC correction. Default: TRUE. remove_uncorrected Logical. TRUE, remove features QCRSC correct. Default: FALSE. spline_smooth Numeric [0, 1]. Smoothing parameter. Default: 0. spline_spar_limit Numeric vector. Lower/upper bounds spline. Default: c(-1.5, 1.5). correct_on_log Logical. TRUE, fit log-transformed data. Default: TRUE. min_qc_per_batch Integer. Minimum QC samples per batch. Default: 5. normalize_method Character character vector. One normalization methods apply. multiple methods supplied every method paired every element transform_method (Cartesian product). "sum": Normalizes sum. "median": Normalizes median. "specific_factor": Uses external factors norm_factor_col. "pqn_global": PQN using global reference. "pqn_reference": PQN using normalize_ref_sample. "pqn_group": PQN using pooled QC samples reference. "quantile": Quantile normalization. "none": normalization. Default: "median". normalize_ref_sample Character. Reference sample name PQN. Default: NULL. normalize_qc_method Character. QC normalization using factors. One "mean", "median", "none". Default: "none". transform_method Character character vector. One transformation methods apply. multiple methods supplied every method paired every element normalize_method (Cartesian product). "log2", "log10", "sqrt", "cbrt", "clr", "vsn", "glog", "none". Default: "log10". vsn_cores Integer "max". Cores VSN. Default: 1. scale_nonpls Character. Scaling NONPLS branch ("auto", "pareto", "mean"). scale_pls Character. Scaling PLS branch ("pareto", "auto", "mean"). rsd_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. QC-RSD threshold. Default: 0.3. rsd_qc_type Character. QC type RSD ("EQC", "SQC", "QC"). variance_percentile Numeric [0, 100]. Percentile filter. Default: 10. scale_filter_ref Character. Harmonisation strategy. "auto": Keep intersection branches. "NONPLS": Apply NONPLS filters . "PLS": Apply PLS filters . merge_replicates Logical. Average technical replicates. Default: FALSE. track Character vector NULL. Feature names monitor across preprocessing steps. tracked feature removed, step reason (e.g., missingness percentage, RSD value) recorded. Default: NULL. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default: TRUE.

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: Single combination (scalar normalize_method scalar transform_method) named list class run_DIpreprocess - identical original behaviour. Multiple combinations (either argument vector length > 1) named list class run_DIpreprocess_multi.  element named <normalize>_<transform> (e.g. sum_log10) run_DIpreprocess object.  shared pre-normalization data (post-drift-correction) stored shared attribute.

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: Preprocessing Workflow (Fixed Order): Input validation Outlier removal Missing-value filtering (run_filtermissing) Missing-value imputation (run_mvimpute) Signal drift correction (run_driftBatchCorrect) [Per combination] Normalization (run_normalize) [Per combination] Transformation (run_transform) [Per combination] Scaling (run_scale) [Per combination] Quality filtering (run_filterRSD, run_filtervariance) [Per combination] Common-feature harmonisation & replicate merging

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: Zero Handling: pipeline automatically converts 0 values x NA prior analysis. Multi-method pre-flight checks: vectors supplied normalize_method transform_method, function validates method-specific requirements (e.g. norm_factor_col "specific_factor", normalize_ref_sample "pqn_reference") executing preprocessing step, errors surfaced early rather mid-run.

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: Kirwan, J.., et al. (2013). Analytical Bioanalytical Chemistry, 405, 5147-5157.

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Direct-Injection Metabolomics Preprocessing Pipeline — run_DIpreprocess
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) n_s <- 80; n_f <- 100 x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(n_s * n_f, 500, 150)), nrow = n_s, ncol = n_f) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", seq_len(n_f)) x[sample(length(x), 400)] <- 0  meta <- data.frame(   Sample            = paste0("S", seq_len(n_s)),   Group             = rep(c("Control", "Treatment", "QC"), c(30, 30, 20)),   Batch             = rep(1:2, each = 40),   InjectionSequence = seq_len(n_s),   SubjectID         = c(paste0("BIO", seq_len(60)), rep(NA, 20)),   stringsAsFactors  = FALSE ) rownames(x) <- meta$Sample  # --- Single combination (original behaviour) --- result_single <- run_DIpreprocess(   x                = x,   metadata         = meta,   normalize_method = "pqn_group",   transform_method = "log2",   correct_drift    = FALSE ) class(result_single)          # "run_DIpreprocess" dim(result_single$data_nonpls)  # --- Multiple transform methods --- result_multi <- run_DIpreprocess(   x                = x,   metadata         = meta,   normalize_method = "median",   transform_method = c("log10", "vsn"),   correct_drift    = FALSE ) class(result_multi)           # "run_DIpreprocess_multi" names(result_multi)           # "median_log10"  "median_vsn"  # --- Full Cartesian product --- result_cart <- run_DIpreprocess(   x                = x,   metadata         = meta,   normalize_method = c("sum", "median"),   transform_method = c("log10", "vsn"),   correct_drift    = FALSE ) names(result_cart) # "sum_log10"  "sum_vsn"  "median_log10"  "median_vsn" } # }

what: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: Applies Quality Control-based Robust Spline Correction (QCRSC) batch correction using ComBat remove systematic signal drift batch effects metabolomics data.

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
code: run_driftBatchCorrect(   x,   metadata,   perform_correction = TRUE,   batch_corr_only = FALSE,   injection_col = "InjectionSequence",   batch_col = "Batch",   group_col = "Group",   qc_label = "QC",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   spline_smooth_param = 0,   min_QC = 5,   spar_limit = c(-1.5, 1.5),   log_scale = TRUE,   use_parametric = TRUE,   display_plots = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'run_driftBatchCorrect' print(x, ...)

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: x Object print. metadata Data frame. Sample metadata number rows equal nrow(x). perform_correction Logical. TRUE, perform correction; FALSE, return original data unchanged. Default: TRUE. batch_corr_only Logical. TRUE, perform ComBat batch correction (drift correction). FALSE, perform QCRSC. Default: FALSE. injection_col Character. Column metadata containing injection order. Default: "InjectionSequence". batch_col Character. Column metadata containing batch numbers. Default: "Batch". group_col Character. Column metadata containing sample group labels. Default: "Group". qc_label Character. Group label identifying QC samples QCRSC. Default: "QC". qc_types Character vector. Group labels converted qc_label internally. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). spline_smooth_param Numeric. Smoothing parameter (0-1). Default: 0. min_QC Integer. Minimum QC samples per batch. Default: 5. spar_limit Numeric vector length 2. Spline limits. Default: c(-1.5, 1.5). log_scale Logical. Fit log-transformed data. Default: TRUE. use_parametric Logical. Parametric ComBat adjustment. Default: TRUE. display_plots Logical. Diagnostic plots ComBat. Default: FALSE. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default: TRUE. ... Ignored.

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: list class "run_driftBatchCorrect" containing: data Corrected data (class input x) data_before_correction Original data correction correction_applied Logical method_used Character uncorrected_features Character vector uncorrected features n_uncorrected Integer parameters List parameters

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: QCRSC fits smoothing splines QC intensities across injection order. Requires pmp. ComBat uses empirical Bayes batch correction. Requires sva.

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: Kirwan, J.., et al. (2013). Analytical Bioanalytical Chemistry, 405, 5147-5157. doi:10.1007/s00216-013-6856-7 Johnson, W.E., et al. (2007). Biostatistics, 8(1), 118-127. doi:10.1093/biostatistics/kxj037

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Correct Signal Drift and Batch Effects Using QC Samples — run_driftBatchCorrect
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(417) n <- 60; p <- 30 x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(n * p, 100, 20)), nrow = n, ncol = p) colnames(x) <- paste0("F", seq_len(p))  meta <- data.frame(   InjectionSequence = seq_len(n),   Batch = rep(1:2, each = 30),   Group = rep(c(rep("Sample", 9), "QC"), 6) )  # Skip correction (returns unchanged) result <- run_driftBatchCorrect(x, meta, perform_correction = FALSE) #> Correction disabled. Returning original data. result #> === Drift/Batch Correction Results === #> Correction applied: FALSE #> Method used: None # }

what: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: Removes features high relative standard deviation (RSD, also known coefficient variation) quality control samples. High RSD indicates poor analytical reproducibility.

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
code: run_filterRSD(   x,   metadata,   max_rsd = 0.3,   group_col = "Group",   qc_type = "EQC",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'run_filterRSD' print(x, ...)

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: x Object print. metadata Data frame. Sample metadata number rows equal nrow(x). Must contain column specified group_col identify QC samples. max_rsd Numeric. Maximum allowed RSD (0-1). Features RSD >= threshold QC samples removed. Default: 0.3 (30%). group_col Character. Name column metadata containing sample group labels. Default: "Group". qc_type Character. QC sample type use RSD calculation. Options: "EQC": Extract QC samples (diluted sample pool) "SQC": Sample QC samples (undiluted sample pool) "QC": QC sample (use EQC SQC present, distinction) Default: "EQC". qc_types Character vector. Group labels treated QC samples. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE. ... Ignored.

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: list class "run_filterRSD" containing: data Matrix data frame filtered data (class input x) features_removed Character vector removed feature names features_kept Character vector retained feature names rsd_values Numeric vector RSD values original features n_features_before Integer. Number features filtering n_features_after Integer. Number features filtering n_features_removed Integer. Number features removed parameters List parameters used

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: Relative Standard Deviation (RSD): RSD calculated : RSD = SD / Mean Also known coefficient variation (CV), RSD expresses variability proportion mean, making scale-independent suitable comparing features different magnitudes. Filter RSD: Quality control samples low variability since represent biological matrix. High RSD QC samples indicates: Poor analytical reproducibility Instrument instability Ion suppression/enhancement effects Features near detection limit Typical RSD Thresholds: Stringent: 20% (0.2) - high-quality data Standard: 30% (0.3) - recommended metabolomics studies Lenient: 40% (0.4) - sample size limited

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: Broadhurst, D.. (2025). QC:MXP Repeat Injection based Quality Control, Batch Correction, Exploration & Data Cleaning (Version 2.1) Zendono. doi:10.5281/zenodo.16824822 . Retrieved https://github.com/broadhurstdavid/QC-MXP. Jankevics , Lloyd GR, Weber RJM (2025). pmp: Peak Matrix Processing signal batch correction metabolomics datasets. doi:10.18129/B9.bioc.pmp , R package version 1.20.0, https://bioconductor.org/packages/pmp.

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Filter Features by Relative Standard Deviation in QC Samples — run_filterRSD
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(814) x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100, sd = 20)),             nrow = 100, ncol = 50) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  metadata <- data.frame(   Sample = paste0("S", 1:100),   Group = c(rep("Control", 40), rep("Treatment", 40),             rep("QC", 10), rep("EQC", 10)) )  result <- run_filterRSD(x, metadata, max_rsd = 0.3, qc_type = "EQC") #> Filtering features by RSD (threshold: 30.0%, QC type: EQC)... #> Removed 1 features (2.0%) with RSD >= 30.0% result #> === RSD Filtering Results === #> Features before: 50 #> Features after:  49 #> Features removed: 1 (2.0%) #> RSD threshold: 30.0% #> QC type used: EQC # }

what: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
text: Removes features (columns) exceed specified missing value threshold. Can assess missingness globally group, optional inclusion QC samples. Supports treating zero values missing, common metabolomics data.

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
code: run_filtermissing(   x,   metadata,   threshold = 0.2,   filter_by_group = TRUE,   any_group = TRUE,   include_QC = FALSE,   group_col = "Group",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   zero_as_missing = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'run_filtermissing' print(x, ...)

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
text: x Object print. metadata Data frame. Sample metadata number rows equal nrow(x). Must contain column specified group_col filter_by_group = TRUE. threshold Numeric. Maximum proportion missing values allowed (0-1). Features missing proportion >= threshold removed. Default: 0.2 (20%). filter_by_group Logical. TRUE, assess missingness within group separately. feature kept passes threshold least one group. Default: TRUE. any_group Logical. used filter_by_group = TRUE. TRUE (default), feature retained missingness threshold one group. implements "Modified 80% Rule" (Yang et al., 2015). FALSE, feature must satisfy threshold groups. include_QC Logical. TRUE, include QC samples missingness calculation. Default: FALSE (QC samples excluded assessment). group_col Character. Name column metadata containing group information. Required filter_by_group = TRUE. Default: "Group". qc_types Character vector. Group labels identify QC samples. samples excluded missingness calculation include_QC = FALSE. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). zero_as_missing Logical. TRUE, treat zero values missing (NA) applying filter. common metabolomics zeros often represent values detection limit rather true zeros. Default: TRUE. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE. ... Ignored.

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
text: list class "run_filtermissing" containing: data Matrix data frame filtered data (class input x) features_removed Character vector removed feature names features_kept Character vector retained feature names n_features_before Integer. Number features filtering n_features_after Integer. Number features filtering n_features_removed Integer. Number features removed n_zeros_converted Integer. Number zero values converted NA (applicable) parameters List parameters used missingness_summary Data frame per-feature missingness statistics

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
text: function filters features based proportion missing values (NAs). Zero values treated missing metabolomics data, zero values often represent one following: Peak intensities detection limit Failed peak integration Metabolites detected sample zero_as_missing = TRUE (default), zero values converted missing values filtering. recommended untargeted metabolomics datasets. Set zero_as_missing = FALSE one following true: preprocessing pipeline already converted zeros NA Zero represents true biological absence quantitative meaning dataset contains negative values (example baseline correction) Filtering strategies Two filtering modes available: Global filtering (filter_by_group = FALSE): Missingness calculated across eligible samples. Features missing proportion greater equal threshold removed. threshold = 0.20, essentially, implementing procedure Smilde et al. (2005), 80% rule, .e., variable kept non-zero value least 80% samples. Group-wise filtering (filter_by_group = TRUE): Missingness calculated separately within group. default (any_group = TRUE), feature retained satisfies threshold least one group. implements "Modified 80% Rule" (Yang et al., 2015), less stringent helps preserve group-specific features. Set any_group = FALSE require threshold met across groups. QC sample handling include_QC = FALSE, samples whose group labels match qc_types excluded missingness calculations. recommended QC samples often exhibit different missingness patterns compared biological samples.

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
text: Broadhurst, D.. (2025). QC:MXP Repeat Injection based Quality Control, Batch Correction, Exploration & Data Cleaning (Version 2.1) Zendono. doi:10.5281/zenodo.16824822 . Retrieved https://github.com/broadhurstdavid/QC-MXP. Smilde, . K., van der Werf, M. J., Bijlsma, S., van der Werff-van der Vat, B. J., & Jellema, R. H. (2005). Fusion mass spectrometry-based metabolomics data. Anal. Chem. 77, 6729-6736. doi:10.1021/ac051080y . Wei, R., Wang, J., Su, M., Jia, E., Chen, S., Chen, T., & Ni, Y. (2018). Missing Value Imputation Approach Mass Spectrometry-based Metabolomics Data. Scientific Reports, 8(1), 663. doi:10.1038/s41598-017-19120-0 Yang J, Zhao X, Lu X, Lin X Xu G (2015) data preprocessing strategy metabolomics reduce mask effect data analysis. Front. Mol. Biosci. 2:4. doi:10.3389/fmolb.2015.00004

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Filter Features by Missing Value Threshold — run_filtermissing
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(302) x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100)), nrow = 100, ncol = 50) x[sample(length(x), 300)] <- 0 x[sample(length(x), 200)] <- NA colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  metadata <- data.frame(   Sample = paste0("S", 1:100),   Group = rep(c("Control", "Treatment", "QC"), c(40, 40, 20)) )  result <- run_filtermissing(x, metadata, threshold = 0.3) #> Starting missing value filtering (threshold: 30.0%)... #> Converted 286 zero values to NA (zero_as_missing = TRUE) #> Removed 0 features (0.0%) with missingness >= 30.0% result #> === Missing Value Filtering Results === #> Features before: 50 #> Features after:  50 #> Features removed: 0 (0.0%) #> Threshold: 30.0% #> Mode: Any group(s) must pass #> Zeros converted to NA: 286 # }

what: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
title: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
text: Removes features low variance across samples. Features minimal variation provide little information downstream analysis can safely removed reduce dimensionality.

title: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
code: run_filtervariance(x, percentile = 10, verbose = TRUE)  # S3 method for class 'run_filtervariance' print(x, ...)

title: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
text: x Object print. percentile Numeric. Percentile threshold (0-100) variance filtering. Features bottom X percentile variance removed. example, 10 removes 10% features lowest variance. Default: 10. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE. ... Ignored.

title: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
text: list class "run_filtervariance" containing: data Matrix data frame filtered data (class input x) features_removed Character vector removed feature names features_kept Character vector retained feature names variance_values Numeric vector variance values original features variance_threshold Numeric. variance value corresponding percentile cutoff n_features_before Integer. Number features filtering n_features_after Integer. Number features filtering n_features_removed Integer. Number features removed parameters List parameters used

title: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
text: Filter Variance: Features low variance across samples: Provide minimal discriminatory power classification Contribute little multivariate models (PCA, PLS-DA) May represent technical noise detection artifacts Increase computational burden without adding information Choosing Percentile: Conservative (5-10%): Remove least variable features Moderate (10-20%): Standard approach studies Aggressive (20-30%): high dimensionality concern

title: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
text: Broadhurst, D.. (2025). QC:MXP Repeat Injection based Quality Control, Batch Correction, Exploration & Data Cleaning (Version 2.1) Zendono. doi:10.5281/zenodo.16824822 . Retrieved https://github.com/broadhurstdavid/QC-MXP.

title: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Filter Features by Low Variance — run_filtervariance
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(519) x <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100, sd = seq(0.1, 25, length.out = 50)),             nrow = 100, ncol = 50, byrow = TRUE) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  result <- run_filtervariance(x, percentile = 10) #> Filtering features by variance (10th percentile cutoff)... #> Removed 5 features (10.0%) with variance <= 6.884355 result #> === Variance Filtering Results === #> Features before: 50 #> Features after:  45 #> Features removed: 5 (10.0%) #> Percentile cutoff: 10% #> Variance threshold: 6.884355 # }

what: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
title: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
text: Replaces missing values (NAs) using either simple deterministic method (fraction minimum), statistical imputation method (quantile regression imputation left-censored data, QRILC), method available mice (Multiple Imputation Chained Equations).

title: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
code: run_mvimpute(   x,   method = 0.2,   positive_only = TRUE,   tune_sigma = 1,   m = 5,   maxit = 5,   seed = NA,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'run_mvimpute' print(x, ...)

title: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
text: x Object print. method Numeric character. Controls imputation strategy: Numeric – fraction smallest observed value per feature used deterministic imputation (e.g., 0.2 = 1/5th smallest value). default (positive_only = TRUE), minimum computed strictly positive values . "quantileregression" – uses QRILC algorithm imputeLCMD package. Requires imputeLCMD. mice method string – e.g., "pmm", "norm", "rf", "cart", etc. Requires mice. "none" – Skips imputation entirely. Default: 0.2. positive_only Logical. used method numeric. TRUE (default), per-feature minimum computed strictly positive values. tune_sigma Numeric. used method = "quantileregression". Controls standard deviation left-censored distribution. Default: 1. m Integer. used mice methods. Number imputations. Default: 5. maxit Integer. used mice methods. Number iterations. Default: 5. seed Integer NA. used mice methods. Random seed. Default: NA. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE. ... Ignored.

title: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
text: list class "run_mvimpute" containing: data Matrix data frame imputed values (class input x). n_missing_before Integer. Number missing values imputation. n_missing_after Integer. Number missing values imputation. imputed_summary Data frame per-feature imputation statistics. parameters List parameters used. method_used Character string describing imputation method applied.

title: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
text: Missing Value Imputation Strategies: Deterministic (fraction minimum): missing value replaced fraction per-feature minimum. Standard metabolomics MNAR data. Quantile Regression (QRILC): Models missing values left-censored distribution. Requires imputeLCMD. mice: Multiple Imputation Chained Equations. Requires mice.

title: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
text: Van Buuren, S., & Groothuis-Oudshoorn, K. (2011). mice: Multivariate Imputation Chained Equations R. Journal Statistical Software, 45(3), 1-67. doi:10.18637/jss.v045.i03 Wei, R., et al. (2018). Missing Value Imputation Approach Mass Spectrometry-based Metabolomics Data. Scientific Reports, 8(1), 663. doi:10.1038/s41598-017-19120-0

title: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Impute Missing Values in Metabolomics Data — run_mvimpute
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(723) x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100)), nrow = 100, ncol = 50) x[sample(length(x), 200)] <- NA colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  result <- run_mvimpute(x, method = 0.2) #> Starting missing value imputation (200 missing values)... #> Using deterministic imputation: 0.2000 x minimum positive value per feature. #> Imputation complete. Missing values: 200 -> 0. result #> === Missing Value Imputation Results === #> Method: Deterministic: 0.2000 x min positive #> Missing values before: 200 #> Missing values after:  0 # }

title: Normalize Metabolomics Data — run_normalize

title: Normalize Metabolomics Data — run_normalize
text: x Object print. metadata Data frame. Sample metadata number rows equal nrow(x). method Character numeric vector. Normalization method: "sum": Total sum normalization "median": Median normalization "specific_factor": Use values metadata column "pqn_global": PQN using global median "pqn_reference": PQN using specific reference sample "pqn_group": PQN using pooled QC samples reference "pqn": PQN via pmp package (requires pmp) "quantile": Alias "pqn" (PQN via pmp) "col_rel_abundance": Relative abundance per column (feature) "row_rel_abundance": Relative abundance per row (sample) Numeric vector: Custom normalization factors factor_col Character. Metadata column normalization factors. Default: "Normalization". ref_sample Character. Reference sample PQN. Default: NULL. group_sample Character. Group label PQN group reference. Default: "QC". qc_normalize Character. QC normalization strategy: "mean", "median", "none". Default: "median". group_col Character. Column group labels. Default: "Group". qc_types Character vector. QC group labels. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). reference_method Character. Reference method PQN via pmp: "mean" "median". Default: "mean". sample_id_col Character. Column sample identifiers. Default: "Sample". verbose Logical. Print progress. Default: TRUE. ... Ignored.

text: list class "run_normalize" containing: data Normalized data (class input x) normalization_factors Numeric vector factors used method_used Character describing method parameters List parameters

title: Normalize Metabolomics Data — run_normalize
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Normalize Metabolomics Data — run_normalize
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(215) x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(80 * 40, mean = 100, sd = 20)), nrow = 80, ncol = 40) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:40) meta <- data.frame(   Sample = paste0("S", 1:80),   Group = rep(c("Control", "Treatment", "QC"), c(30, 30, 20)) )  result <- run_normalize(x, meta, method = "median") #> Applying 'median' normalization... #> Normalizing by median... #> Normalization complete. result #> === Normalization Results === #> Method: median #> Data dimensions: 80 samples x 40 features # }

title: Scale Metabolomics Data — run_scale
text: Applies various scaling methods standardize features prepare data multivariate analysis. Includes classical scaling methods well variance-stability (VAST) scaling variants.

title: Scale Metabolomics Data — run_scale
code: run_scale(   x,   method = "auto",   metadata = NULL,   group_col = "Group",   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'run_scale' print(x, ...)

title: Scale Metabolomics Data — run_scale
text: x Object print. method Character. Scaling method: "mean": Mean-centering "auto": Auto-scaling (mean-centering + unit variance) "pareto": Pareto-scaling (mean-centering + sqrt(SD)) "ln": Natural log transformation "vast": VAST scaling (auto-scaling weighted CV) "svast": Supervised VAST (weighted mean class CV) "xvast": Extended supervised VAST (weighted max class CV) "none": scaling metadata Data frame. Required "svast" "xvast". Default: NULL. group_col Character. Column metadata group labels. Required supervised methods. Default: "Group". verbose Logical. Print progress. Default: TRUE. ... Ignored.

title: Scale Metabolomics Data — run_scale
text: list class "run_scale" containing: data Scaled data (class input x) scaling_factors Numeric vector per-feature scaling factors center_values Numeric vector per-feature means method_used Character parameters List parameters

title: Scale Metabolomics Data — run_scale
text: auto: Recommended PCA, clustering, t-tests, ANOVA. pareto: Recommended PLS-DA, OPLS-DA. vast/svast/xvast: Variance-stability methods Keun et al. (2003) Yang et al. (2015).

title: Scale Metabolomics Data — run_scale
text: van den Berg, R.., et al. (2006). BMC Genomics, 7, 142. doi:10.1186/1471-2164-7-142 Yang, J., et al. (2015). Frontiers Molecular Biosciences, 2, 4. doi:10.3389/fmolb.2015.00004

title: Scale Metabolomics Data — run_scale
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(662) x <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 50, mean = 100, sd = 50), nrow = 100, ncol = 50) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:50)  result <- run_scale(x, method = "auto") #> Applying 'auto' scaling... #> Scaling complete. result #> === Scaling Results === #> Method  : auto #> Data    : 100 samples x 50 features # }

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: Applies various transformation methods stabilize variance reduce heteroscedasticity metabolomics data.

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
code: run_transform(   x,   method = "log2",   metadata = NULL,   group_col = "Group",   qc_types = c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"),   num_cores = 1,   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'run_transform' print(x, ...)

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: x Object print. method Character. Transformation method apply. Options: "log2": Log base 2 transformation "log10": Log base 10 transformation "sqrt": Square root transformation "cbrt": Cube root transformation "clr": Centered log-ratio transformation (computed per sample) "arcsin_sqrt": Arcsine square root transformation (data must [0, 1]) "vsn": Variance Stabilizing Normalization (requires vsn) "glog": Generalized logarithm transformation (requires pmp) metadata Data frame. Sample metadata, required glog. Default: NULL. group_col Character. Column metadata group labels. Default: "Group". qc_types Character vector. QC group labels. Default: c("QC", "SQC", "EQC"). num_cores Integer "max". Cores VSN. Default: 1. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default: TRUE. ... Ignored.

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: list class "run_transform" containing: data Transformed data (class input x) method_used Character describing method shift_applied Numeric shift added transformation parameters List parameters

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: log2/log10: Logarithmic compression. shift added non-positive values. sqrt/cbrt: Power transformations. shift added negative values. clr: Centered log-ratio. Maps compositional data real space. arcsin_sqrt: proportion data [0, 1]. vsn: Model-based variance stabilization. Requires vsn BiocParallel. glog: Generalized logarithm via pmp. Requires metadata QC labels.

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: Huber, W., et al. (2002). Bioinformatics, 18(Suppl 1), S96-S104. doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/18.suppl_1.s96 Parsons, H.M., et al. (2007). BMC Bioinformatics, 8, 234. doi:10.1186/1471-2105-8-234

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Transform Metabolomics Data — run_transform
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(937) x <- matrix(abs(rnorm(80 * 40, mean = 100, sd = 50)), nrow = 80, ncol = 40) colnames(x) <- paste0("Feature", 1:40)  result <- run_transform(x, method = "log2") #> Applying 'log2' transformation... #> Transformation complete. result #> === Transformation Results === #> Method: log2 #> Data dimensions: 80 samples x 40 features # }
